prompt: The Consumer modifies a local variable but the caller sees no change. What is the bug?
choices: Consumer.accept is non-blocking and runs asynchronously
Lambda expressions can only capture effectively final local variables; count++ modifies count, making it not effectively final — the code does not compile
Consumer.accept returns void so the count change is discarded
int variables cannot be captured by lambdas; use Integer instead
explanation: Lambdas can only capture local variables that are effectively final (never reassigned). count++ modifies count, so it is not effectively final — the compiler rejects the lambda. Fix: use an int[] counter = {0}; so you mutate counter[0] (the array reference is final) or use AtomicInteger.
